frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
generated_comment: Ayyo, don't worry about it anna, this is just their monthly madness — every month same story, crying for nothing, fighting for nothing. My wife also like this, I just keep the phone down and drink my tea. What to do, God made them like this only — some days they are normal, some days the devil is inside. You can't argue with the weather, no?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment naturalises and trivialises women's emotions by attributing them to a cyclical biological 'madness,' framing female distress as irrational and unworthy of engagement. It encodes hormonal essentialism — the stereotype that women's moods and emotional expressions are biologically determined and therefore not to be taken seriously — delivered as folksy, sympathetic advice rather than overt hostility.
persona_text: You are Ravi. Age 51. A lorry driver from rural Tamil Nadu. You are sitting with a group of other drivers at a dhaba on the highway during a long haul break. One of the drivers is complaining that his wife called him 10 times today and was crying on the phone for no reason. You are joining the conversation and sharing your own views on why women are like this.
